compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Coaching-move suggestion (from live use): plan-review has a pass-4 COACH that emits a productive next-move per finding. When a finding asserts a symbol/library-fact is absent AND the referenced name looks like it could be a third-party library symbol (CamelCase, zero rebar-repo hits, plausibly under an installed dependency's namespace), the coach should suggest the EVIDENCE-INJECTION move: 'Run a small offline experiment that proves the library behavior (import the symbol, show its MRO / a canned-response propagation) and paste the captured output into the ticket description (a `## Verified library behavior (experiment log)` section). The verifier pass then has concrete, in-context evidence to DROP the absence finding.' This makes the gate self-healing against the repo-scoped-read blind spot rather than dead-ending the author in an unremediable BLOCK. Empirically observed to work: embedding a captured pydantic_ai.exceptions experiment log (showing ContentFilterError/IncompleteToolCall resolve as UnexpectedModelBehavior subclasses) gave the verifier grounds to drop the false 'symbol does not exist' findings. Consider adding this as a named coaching template keyed on the absence-finding + third-party-symbol heuristic. Also fold the experiment-log convention into docs/plan-review-gate.md so authors know the move exists.

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Author a ticket plan (or code diff) that references a valid THIRD-PARTY library symbol which has zero occurrences in the rebar repo â€” e.g. `pydantic_ai.exceptions.ContentFilterError` or `IncompleteToolCall` (both importable at pydantic-ai 1.107.0; reproduce: `python -c 'from pydantic_ai.exceptions import ContentFilterError, IncompleteToolCall'` exits 0).
2. Run `rebar review-plan <id>` (or the code-review gate).
3. The LLM reviewer emits BLOCK findings across multiple criteria (observed on E4/G1G2/G6/T1/T8) asserting the symbol 'does not exist anywhere in the rebar codebase or in pydantic-ai's exception surface' / 'produces zero search hits'.

## Observed contrast (discriminating evidence)
- Symbols that DO appear in the rebar repo are NOT flagged: `ModelHTTPError` and `UsageLimitExceeded` (both imported in `src/rebar/llm/runner.py`) pass, while `ContentFilterError`/`IncompleteToolCall` (equally valid, but absent from rebar src/tests) are flagged absent. The trigger is 'referenced symbol has zero occurrences in the rebar repo', not 'symbol is actually absent'.
- No amount of prose remediation (inline reproduce-commands, 'this is a library symbol' notes) clears it; the finding recurs every re-review. This is the exact 'code-blind reviewer' false-positive the epic jira-reb-687 pre-emptively documented for the `[retries]` extra.

## Root cause
The DET grounding ORACLE is correct: `src/rebar/grounding/oracle.py:refute_absence` routes a `symbol`/`import`/`member` reference to the T1 ctags lane and, per its contract, 'NEVER asserts an absence' â€” it ABSTAINS when it cannot resolve a symbol. The false-positive originates in the LLM reviewer TIER (plan-review pass-1 finder / pass-2 verifier), whose code-read file tools are scoped to the rebar repo snapshot (`pai_tools.filesystem_tools(cfg.repo_path)`, `src/rebar/llm/runner.py:218`). The reviewer cannot read the installed third-party packages in the venv's site-packages, so it reasons 'I searched the codebase, found no such symbol, therefore it does not exist' and emits a BLOCK â€” unbounded by the oracle's abstain discipline. Third-party library symbols are structurally invisible to the reviewer, so every plan/diff that references one risks a false BLOCK.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: a referenced symbol the reviewer cannot locate in the repo but which MAY be a third-party library symbol is treated as UNVERIFIABLE (abstain / no finding), mirroring the grounding oracle's 'never assert absence' discipline â€” not asserted absent.
- Actual: the LLM reviewer asserts the symbol is nonexistent and BLOCKs, producing unremediable false-positive gate failures for any plan referencing a valid library symbol.

## Impact
Blocks legitimate plans/diffs whenever they name a third-party symbol absent from the rebar repo; forces either lossy rephrasing (removing precise, correct symbol references from plans) or `--force` claim bypass. Degrades the plan-review gate's trustworthiness (false BLOCKs) and wastes ~10-min review cycles per iteration.

## Suggested fix directions (pick one, design first)
- Route the reviewer's symbol-existence claims through the grounding oracle (which abstains), so the LLM cannot directly assert absence of a symbol.
- Give the reviewer a bounded symbol-resolution tool over the RUNTIME environment (`importlib.util.find_spec` / import probe / ctags over installed deps), so third-party symbols resolve.
- Prompt-instruct the finder/verifier to ABSTAIN (not refute) on a symbol it cannot locate in-repo when it could plausibly be a third-party library symbol, mirroring `oracle.refute_absence`'s 'never assert absence' contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A plan/diff that references a valid third-party library symbol absent from the rebar repo (e.g. `ContentFilterError`) does NOT produce a 'symbol does not exist' BLOCK finding.
- [ ] The reviewer either resolves the symbol against the runtime environment or abstains â€” it never asserts absence of a symbol it merely cannot see in the repo snapshot.
- [ ] A regression test injects a plan referencing a known-importable third-party symbol and asserts no absence-BLOCK is produced.
- [ ] Symbols genuinely absent everywhere (repo AND runtime) still surface (no loss of true-positive absence detection).
- [ ] make lint && make typecheck && make test green.
compiled_state.title: Plan/code-review LLM grounding false-positives 'symbol does not exist' on third-party library symbols (repo-scoped read tools can't see site-packages)
